title: Traveling with Kyle - Peru Part 4/7 - Cuzco

transcript.text: Hello, so this video is traveling with Kyle, Peru, so when I left off, I got done with the Inca Trail, so then I go back to Kuzco and I, Fruitsi, go back to my hotel room, hotel, it's a cool hotel because it had these movies in this movie theater place upstairs every night, you know, and stuff, and I was like right near some little really narrow pedestrian kind of area, just north of the central plaza where there was lots of Mexican people.

I'm all into Mexican food, so I was eating all the different Mexican restaurants, and I think they even had like margaritas or something, and then they also had this weird place, I've never actually been to a place like that, north side of the plaza where you could like grab a movie and go into a certain room and watch it, so I did that a couple times, you know, and you know, I was back on, I didn't bring, thank God, I didn't bring my marijuana cookies on my trip, I wanted to take a couple days off, but then I started eating them again when I came back, you know, and I started, that's when I kind of started to realize that I was starting to like lose my mind,

And lose my body as well, like I was, I couldn't get up earlier than 12, I couldn't, I was like in comatose before 12, it started to kind of, you know, get a hold of my body and turn me into like melted wax kind of, but I still did some other stuff, you know, I rode my bicycle up to Sacsayhuaman,

Which is, um, on the central plaza of Cusco, even though it's a big city, the central plaza of Cusco is right on the edge of this really steep hill that goes up, and on the top of that hill is Sacsayhuaman, which is this big wall, a couple hundred meters along, that is jagged, like if you look at it from space, it's like a jaguar mouth, and you know,

You can't tell from the ground, but if you look at it from space, it's like an open jaguar, and the teeth with the jaguar mouth, those are these walls of Sacsayhuaman, and when the Spanish came, they tried to take it apart, and they could only take

Four-fifths of it apart, but they couldn't take the rest of it apart, because the stones were so big, the stones were about as big as this room, they're like 300 tons each, and you had them stacked up on top of each other, perfectly stacked, perfectly placed, you know, and some of them even had like different, you know,

Angulars that were putting in there, and there's no way that we could probably even do that today, we could probably move those stones, but probably only a couple of feet, so it's a mystery of how they put it there, I have my own theories, but that's another video,
Um, I had, I even took a photo of my bicycle next to the stone, you know, to show how big they were, and it's just gigantic, I mean, you're talking stones 10 feet high, or whatever,
And that other one's just as big on top of it, and hundreds of them, um, and so yeah, and it was on the edge of this big open field, which they would have, you know, my Inca ceremonies and stuff,
And, uh, and then there was another level of the jaguar teeth, it was kind of like multiple jaguar teeth, kind of like maybe shark's layered teeth or something,
So then it was cool, you go on the second level, and you go up these really steep hills, steep stairs, they even had some like little really steep,
Like this steep, of like stairs you could sit on, with a little plaza thing in the middle, so maybe they had all sorts of different like,
Classrooms, maybe you could call them, I don't know, but, um, and then on the other side of the valley, of that little field,
Up on the top, they had these, um, these terraced, kind of like Olympic stadium, you know, where they give them the Olympic gold medals,
You'd have the top, and then the bottom, but they were carved in granite, and they were carved perfectly flat,
Like six feet wide, and then another one six feet wide, into the granite, perfectly, perfectly, perfectly flat, cut, 90 degree angles,
How the fuck, is an Indian going to do that, even with a metal saw, no, no, I'm talking laser technology,
Anyways, go and look at it, next time you go there, um, I took a photo of that too, my bike on it,
And it was just, it reminded me of what I saw in El Tajin, the northernmost Mayan ruin in Mexico, right now,
Just south of Tampico, so anyways, then I, uh, went, I rode my bike a little bit, um, you know, further down the road,
And there was just other ruins, that was interesting, it was like all these cut rocks, it was like the quarry, I guess,
Where they got certain rocks to build, you know, all the different stones, and stonework, and stuff that they had,

I'm leaving Cusco before, I should have left, stayed in Cusco longer than I was though, a lot longer,
